pattern: Greedily raising each element to just one more than its predecessor whenever it is too small accumulates the fewest increments needed for strict growth.
hints: Walk left to right and never look backward; each element only has to beat the one before it.
If the current value is already greater than the previous, leave it; otherwise it must become previous plus one.
Track the running previous value; cost += max(0, prev + 1 - nums[i]), then set prev to max(nums[i], prev + 1).

edge_inputs.description: already strictly increasing needs zero ops
value drops then recovers above the forced floor
